terms.definition: Receiver Operating Characteristic curve: plot of TPR (recall) vs FPR as the classification threshold on scores varies. Canonical definition in this topic.
Area Under the ROC Curve — single number in [0, 1] summarizing threshold-free ranking quality; equals P(score_positive > score_negative) for random pairs.
TP / (TP + FN); fraction of actual positives correctly flagged at a threshold. Y-axis of the ROC curve; same as recall.
FP / (FP + TN); fraction of actual negatives incorrectly flagged. X-axis of the ROC curve; equals 1 − specificity.
Evaluation that does not fix one classification cutoff — AUC integrates performance across all thresholds on a score ranking.
Measures how well scores order positives above negatives rather than accuracy at one threshold; AUC is the canonical ranking summary here.
A chosen (threshold, TPR, FPR) on the ROC curve used for deployment after model selection via AUC.
